$comment: ELF64 POSITION-INDEPENDENT EXECUTABLE (PIE: ET_DYN + PT_INTERP + entry) for aarch64 Linux -- c171 conformance (the arm64 sibling of elf64-x86_64-linux-pie). CONFIG-ONLY: the PIE arm of `src/link/format/elf.cpp` is arch-agnostic -- `isPie = isDyn && processExit.has_value()` (entry-cluster PRESENCE, not a new elf.type value), DF_1_PIE + the interpreter are read from this schema, and the synthesized `_start` trampoline is the shared, fully target-driven `entry_trampoline.cpp` builder (it reads the CC via `callingConventionByName("aapcs64")` and every instruction via `opcodeByMnemonic` -- the arm64 EXEC already drives this exact trampoline). So this needs NO writer code, only this schema. Like the x86_64 PIE: `elf.type = dyn` routes to `encodeElfExecDynamic`; the entry cluster flips the `isPie` sub-mode (PT_PHDR + PT_INTERP + `.interp`, non-zero base-relative e_entry, DT_FLAGS_1 = DF_1_NOW | DF_1_PIE); the kernel execve's it at a randomized base (full-image ASLR under ld-linux-aarch64). Everything else stays the arm64 dyn shape: base-0 VAs the loader slides, R_AARCH64_RELATIVE for internal absolute data slots, symbol-based absolute relocs for extern-address data slots, got-indirect extern DATA imports, real-named .dynsym exports. THE DISCRIMINATOR from the .so sibling (elf64-aarch64-linux-dyn) is ENTRY-CLUSTER PRESENCE, not a new elf.type: a PIE IS ET_DYN. POLICY: a PIE is an EXECUTABLE -- `allowsUndefinedImports()` FALSE. NO tlsAccess/tdata/tbss (thread_local fails loud, D-LK-DYN-TLS-MODEL). Witnessed under qemu-aarch64 / the ubuntu-24.04-arm CI leg.
$dataModelComment: Same LP64 identity as the aarch64 exec/dyn/rel siblings (64-bit Linux).
$bitFieldStrategyComment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT: Linux arm64 (AAPCS64) uses the generic LSB-first bit-field allocation -- same gnu_packed as the aarch64 ELF siblings.
$externCallDispatchComment: Same call-site shape as the arm64 exec image and the .so: the walker synthesizes a 16-byte ARM64 PLT stub per extern FUNCTION (ADRP+LDR+BR through the GOT slot, GLOB_DAT eager-bound) and the call site is a plain direct `BL` to the stub -- pc-relative, slide-safe under the ET_DYN load slide. D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH.
$dataImportBindingComment: extern library DATA objects bind GOT-INDIRECT -- the walker reserves a GOT slot per imported object and emits R_AARCH64_GLOB_DAT against it; ld.so writes the object's address into the slot and the GotIndirect lowering reads it. Copy relocations are the FIXED-BASE ET_EXEC mechanism; validate() rejects copy-relocation on any ET_DYN schema, PIE included.
$supportedDataSectionsComment: rodata (read-only, R+X PT_LOAD #1 -- reloc-FREE items only; a reloc-bearing rodata item would need a load-time write into a read-only page, D-LK-DYN-RODATA-ITEM-RELOC fails loud), data (mutable initialized globals, R+W PT_LOAD #2), bss (zero-init NOBITS tail), relro (reloc-bearing CONST data, c145 -- merged into `.data` so its R_AARCH64_RELATIVE slots land in writable pages). NO tdata/tbss: thread_local fails loud at the MIR->LIR gate / linker section gate (D-LK-DYN-TLS-MODEL).
$artifactProfilesComment: This format SERVES the runnable-console-binary profile (plan 06 AP3) -- a PIE is an executable, so cli, exactly like the ET_EXEC sibling. The lib profile is the .so (elf64-aarch64-linux-dyn).
$entryPointComment: Empty entryPoint defaults the walker to module.functions[0] -- after the linker prepends the synthetic `_start` trampoline (keyed on processExit presence) that IS the trampoline, so e_entry = the trampoline's base-relative VA. The USER entry resolves via module.userEntrySymbol.
$processExitComment: ENTRY CLUSTER member (the PIE discriminator). Identical to the aarch64 ET_EXEC sibling: terminate via libc `exit(3)` by-name-import (STDIO-FLUSH-AT-EXIT). The trampoline emits a DIRECT `BL` to the PLT stub for `exit` (per externCallDispatch=direct-plt) -- pc-relative, slide-safe. libc.so.6 becomes a DT_NEEDED; ld-linux-aarch64 resolves the GOT slot eagerly (DF_1_NOW).
$entryCallingConventionComment: ENTRY CLUSTER member. AAPCS64 -- the user fn's return value (x0) is moved into argGprs[0] = x0 before the exit call. Read via `target.callingConventionByName("aapcs64")` at trampoline-build time.
$processArgsComment: ENTRY CLUSTER member. c88 (D-RUNTIME-MAIN-ARGC-ARGV): AAPCS64 Linux mirrors the SysV stack-vector layout -- argc at [sp+0], the NULL-terminated argv vector IN PLACE at [sp+8]; ld.so transfers control to a PIE's e_entry with this exact layout. The trampoline emits `LDUR x0,[sp,#0]` + `ADD x1, sp, #8` before any SP adjustment and before `BL main`.

elf.$comment: Elf64_Ehdr identity for an arm64 PIE. type = ET_DYN (3) -- a PIE IS a shared object to the kernel/loader; what makes it an EXECUTABLE is the entry cluster + the resulting non-zero e_entry + PT_INTERP. machine = 183 (EM_AARCH64). pageAlign = 4096. interpreter = the PT_INTERP path (/lib/ld-linux-aarch64.so.1 -- the kernel maps ld.so, which relocates the image at a randomized base and jumps to base + e_entry). NO soname (validate-rejected with the entry cluster). bindNow = true: DT_FLAGS_1 = DF_1_NOW | DF_1_PIE.

sections.$comment: Executable code. virtualAddress = 4096 = elf.pageAlign, PINNED by validate() for every ET_DYN schema (base-0 by construction; page zero holds Ehdr + PHT + .interp + dynamic metadata). sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC|SHF_EXECINSTR (6).
sections.name: .text

sections.$comment: Read-only initialized data. Folded into R+X PT_LOAD #1 after .text. virtualAddress = 0 -- computed. A reloc-bearing const global goes to relro instead (c145).
sections.name: .rodata

sections.$comment: Writable initialized data. R+W PT_LOAD #2. virtualAddress = 0 -- computed.
sections.name: .data

sections.$comment: Zero-initialized data. SHT_NOBITS (8); memsz-only tail of PT_LOAD #2. virtualAddress = 0 -- computed.
sections.name: .bss

relocations.$comment: 26-bit PC-relative branch-with-link (BL) -- the extern-call reloc on a PLT stub. Legal in ET_DYN `.text` (pc-relative). kind = 1 = arm64.target.json's `call26`.
relocations.name: R_AARCH64_CALL26

relocations.$comment: ADRP page-base pc-relative (high 21 bits). Legal in `.text` (pc-relative). kind = 2 = `adr_prel_pg_hi21`.
relocations.name: R_AARCH64_ADR_PREL_PG_HI21

relocations.$comment: ADD low-12 absolute (no check) -- the pair with ADRP. kind = 3 = `add_abs_lo12_nc`.
relocations.name: R_AARCH64_ADD_ABS_LO12_NC

relocations.$comment: abs64 -- DATA items only: the walker patches the slot with the base-relative value AND emits an R_AARCH64_RELATIVE (1027) row (the load slide completes the address); an extern-targeted slot emits a symbol-based R_AARCH64_ABS64 row with the slot zeroed. kind = 4 = `abs64`.
relocations.name: R_AARCH64_ABS64
